background: A parallel system consists of 3 independent components, each having 0.95 reliability. The system reliability is calculated using the formula $$R=1-(1-0.95)^3=0.9999$$.

task.query: What is the failure time of the parallel system when the ith component has failure time $T_i$?
task.answer: The parallel system will fail only upon failure of all its components, and the system failure time is $$T_P=\max _{1 \leq i \leq n} T_i$$.

task.query: What is the failure time distribution function of the system?
task.answer: The failure time distribution function of the system is $$F(t)=P\left(T_P \leq t\right)=\prod_{i=1}^{n} F_i(t)$$.

task.query: What is the failure time density of the system?
task.answer: The system failure time density is $$f(t)=\frac{d F}{d t}=\sum_{i=1}^{n} f_i(t) \prod_{\substack{j=1 \\ j \neq i}}^{n} F_j(t)$$.

task.query: What is the reliability function of the system?
How can the system failure rate be obtained?
task.answer: The system failure rate can be obtained as $$r(t)=\frac{f(t)}{R(t)}=\frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n} f_i(t) \prod_{\substack{j=1 \\ j \neq i}}^{n} F_j(t)}{1-\prod_{\substack{j=1 \\ j \neq i}} F_j(t)}$$.

task.query: What is the reliability function of the system when each component has an exponential failure time distribution with constant failure rate $\lambda_i$?
task.answer: If each of the $n$ independent components has an exponential failure time distribution with constant failure rate $\lambda_i$, the system reliability is $$R(t)=1-\prod_{i=1}^n\left(1-e^{-\lambda_i t}\right)$$.

task.query: How can the mean time to failure (MTTF) of the system be calculated?
task.answer: The mean time to failure of the system can be obtained as $$M_{tbf}=\int_0^{\infty} R(t) dt=\int_0^{\infty}\left(1-\prod_{i=1}^n\left(1-e^{-\lambda_i t}\right)\right) dt$$. After simplifications, $$M_{tbf}=\sum_{i=1}^n \sum_{j_i=1}^n \sum_{\substack{j_{i-1}=1 \\ \left\{j_1>j_2>\ldots>j_i\right\}}}^n \ldots \sum_{j_2=1}^n \sum_{j_1=1}^n \frac{(-1)^{i+1}}{\left(\sum_{k=1}^i \lambda_{j_k}\right)}$$.

task.query: What is the MTTF for a 2-component parallel system?
What is the MTTF for a 3-component parallel system?
task.answer: For a 3-component parallel system, the system MTTF is $$M_{tbf}=\frac{1}{\lambda_1}+\frac{1}{\lambda_2}+\frac{1}{\lambda_3}-\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_2}-\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_3}-\frac{1}{\lambda_2+\lambda_3}+\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_2+\lambda_3}$$.
